interpretation: C16 applies the pooled-anchor FCI estimator (P16) to the human-annotator preference data without any LLM judge. With K=3 annotators per pair on 9125 HS2-Preference pairs, the estimator produces a single R̂*_CA that can be compared to the LLM-derived values (HS2 0.407, UF 0.321). A small gap suggests the method generalises beyond LLM-soft-label input; a large gap suggests low-K regime requires stronger theory (motivates Year-1 low-K extension).
